title: Revolution Rebuild Hideaki Anno
wikitext:
[]: Revolution Rebuild: Hideaki Anno             Quote Post by EvaCub » Wed Jan 14, 2009 11:50 am Evangelion, The original Series, Had a big impact on the "anime industry" It broke in deeper complex characthers and a dark storyline and changed the way mecha anime would be done... So Will Rebuild deliver something new to the industry? and if so what?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Jan 14, 2009 12:00 pm I doubt it. I see Rebuild more as Anno et al jumping on the 'old-anime-series-gets-cinematic-remake' bandwagon that has enjoyed considerable success lately (the Zeta Gundam movies did very well, or so I hear). Baring something really mind-blowing from the later installments, I don't expect Rebuild to have a huge impact on the anime biz.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 14, 2009 12:12 pm I'm inclined to agree, but it would be nice if Rebuild is groundbreaking.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Jan 14, 2009 12:29 pm Neon Genesis Evangelion was a pure case of 'Lighting in a Bottle'. It happened to come around at just right the time when the anime industry needed a kick of originality and it just caught on. Anno made a great story and it was made even greater by the drastic changes he had to make and the whole thing just payed off. I was only 10 when it came out but I would assume (making an ass out of u and me) that when Evangelion was coming out fans were expecting a good show but nothing REVOLUTIONARY. It came on as a complete surprise and just went on to become one of the biggest properties in anime history. Lighting in a bottle. With Rebuild Anno now has to somehow LIVE UP to what Eva was. There's all this anticipation for something greater than great (this topic is an example of that). Despite his egotistical claims otherwise, Anno and Rebuild are not going to reshape the anime industry with this project. At the very least Anno can show the other guys than a cinematic anime remake doesn't need to be just redone shot for shot. My big hope is that Anno is just doing because he wants to get his original concept out there. The one from Proposal. Or that he's actually stimulated by the idea of re-doing Eva. And it's not just for money. I'd say with there now being two leisurely years between films and not 3 months (like originally intended) that might be what happened. Anno jumped onto Rebuild to built capital for his new studio- Khara- and intended to knock out a pretty (and profitable) quickie, but somewhere down the line said- "Damn, this is interesting! I need to make this better!"

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Thu Jan 15, 2009 3:19 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: My big hope is that Anno is just doing because he wants to get his original concept out there. The one from Proposal. Or that he's actually stimulated by the idea of re-doing Eva. And it's not just for money. I'd say with there now being two leisurely years between films and not 3 months (like originally intended) that might be what happened. Anno jumped onto Rebuild to built capital for his new studio- Khara- and intended to knock out a pretty (and profitable) quickie, but somewhere down the line said- "Damn, this is interesting! I need to make this better!"

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jan 16, 2009 7:38 am Evangelion was big because no one had done something like that before. Rebuild won't be big in the same way, because someone already did something like that, it was called Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Fri Jan 16, 2009 5:26 pm I don't really understand how it could bring something new to the anime industry when the first episode is already merely a retelling of the original serie. It's hyped, it's good, it's Evangelion, but it was famous and new 14 years ago. And it's better that way I guess.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Jan 16, 2009 11:03 pm it would be so nice if Webuild would "revolutionize" anime as Anno and everyone at Khara would like us to believe don't get me wrong, there's some good titles out there now, but they're often rather obscure, the exact opposite of the Naruto-types (which is GREAT IMO, but still...), the industry could use a boost and GP and S-s kind of finished what I was to further say, so...

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sat Jan 17, 2009 10:24 am I think Anno can do it - he is great director and has delivered one otaku anime perfection, one revolutionary masterpiece and one brilliant live action movie! He can do it!!!1!!!111!! No he can't. Anno, however, is quite right in that after Eva there hasn't been much happening in anime as whole edit; which doesn't mean there haven't been a lot of great shows, just nothing truly new that often.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Sun Jan 18, 2009 3:51 pm Revolution is a bit much to expect from a remake.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Jan 18, 2009 5:14 pm I see Rebuild as something like what the chaps at Gundam is doing. Going on, and on, and on, ad infinitum. But by jove, I like them. (Obviously, there is going to be splits in opinions)

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Jan 19, 2009 8:15 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: My big hope is that Anno is just doing because he wants to get his original concept out there. The one from Proposal.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jan 19, 2009 8:46 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: My big hope is that Anno is just doing because he wants to get his original concept out there. The one from Proposal.

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Tue Jan 20, 2009 11:11 am I have some fears regarding Rebuild 2. The first Rebuild covered only the first 6 episodes, and even though it wasn't a great movie, it succeded as a remake. Rebuild 2 will cover eps. 7-19, seriosly, how's that possible with a 1 and a half hour movie? I know, many of the episodes are downright useless and will be cut, but the the battles against EVA-03, the giant ball, and the turtle-looking angel (sorry, I can't remenber their names XD) look pretty much confirmed, and they will also introduce new characters and Evangelions. How will they manage to make it as psycological as the original series? Will it become a standard mech anime? And don't forget Rebuild 3, that will likely cover eps. 20-24 in 45 minutes (every single one of these episodes are important and cannot get cut) and Rebuild 4, that will have to be a 45 minutes ending (its possible to make EoE this short, but by taking out all the psycological elements of the movie). I don't have many hopes on Rebuild, the only thing I really want to see is the new ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Jan 20, 2009 12:07 pm HENRCAST wrote: I have some fears regarding Rebuild 2. The first Rebuild covered only the first 6 episodes, and even though it wasn't a great movie, it succeded as a remake. Rebuild 2 will cover eps. 7-19, seriosly, how's that possible with a 1 and a half hour movie? etc, usw...

[]:             Quote Post by HENRCAST » Wed Jan 21, 2009 2:25 pm Action_Bastard wrote: HENRCAST wrote: I have some fears regarding Rebuild 2. The first Rebuild covered only the first 6 episodes, and even though it wasn't a great movie, it succeded as a remake. Rebuild 2 will cover eps. 7-19, seriosly, how's that possible with a 1 and a half hour movie? etc, usw...

[]:             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Wed Jan 21, 2009 9:25 pm What are some revolutionary anime and why are they revolutionary? I know Akira and Cowboy Bebop are some, and maybe Spirited Away or Ghost in the Shell but other than that I don't know much about anime history. What I thought Rebuild did okay but not good enough was making Eva watchable for people who don't watch anime, like the two movies I named above. Rebuild would draw less reactions of "what the fuck is this shit" or "that's cornball/stupid/Japanese cartoon for you" compared to the original series, but it still has some of those typical anime quirks not found in the more internationally accepted works. I would have liked, being a remaking, for Rebuild to be as tolerable and consistently serious as Akira (no fan service), although it has improved over the original series. It might not be as entertaining, but we can just go back to the old show. Evangelion is famous, but Rebuild doesn't seem to be revolutionary. But if it was like Akira, it would get more exposure and maybe considered revolutionary (in the west, that is). But what other things make an anime revolutionary? not sure if this is a thread jack and warrants a new one

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Wed Jan 21, 2009 11:43 pm HENRCAST wrote: No, the one that EVA 01 eats in ep.19
